component_details.name: BeraChef contract (Smart Contract)
component_details.description: Smart contract related to Reward Vault whitelisting.

behavior_details.name: Stake BERA
behavior_details.description: Validator stakes BERA to participate in consensus.

data_flow_details.description: Validator stakes BERA on the chain.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Off-chain proof used for claiming incentives may be vulnerable to spoofing or tampering, allowing unauthorized claims.

actor_risk.description: An external user with potential malicious intent interacting with the claiming mechanism.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The claiming process relies on off-chain data which can be a point of compromise if not securely generated and verified.

assumptions: The off-chain proof generation or storage system is vulnerable to unauthorized access or modification.
The on-chain claiming contract relies solely on the provided proof without sufficient on-chain cross-validation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement stronger cryptographic verification of off-chain proofs tied to on-chain actions, or move proof generation/validation fully on-chain if feasible.

description: An attacker may spoof an off-chain proof to claim incentive tokens belonging to other users who boosted validators.

component_details.name: Governance Contract
component_details.description: Smart contract managing governance proposals, voting, and delegation.

behavior_details.name: Collect Fees/MEV
behavior_details.description: Validator collects transaction fees and MEV and sends them to the FeeCollector.

data_flow_details.description: Incentive distributor sends commission to the validator.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Frequent or malicious staking/withdrawal actions might disrupt the reward vault's functionality or calculations.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to grief the reward vault.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Reward vaults relying on dynamic state (like total supply for APR) might be vulnerable to manipulation disrupting availability or perceived yield.

assumptions: The reward vault contract logic does not adequately prevent griefing attacks through frequent low-value deposits/withdrawals.
Calculations based on total supply or dynamic state are susceptible to manipulation affecting others.

mitigation.strategy: Implement minimum deposit/withdrawal amounts, add fees for frequent interactions, and ensure calculations are robust against state changes.

description: An attacker may perform griefing attacks on a reward vault by repeatedly staking and withdrawing small amounts, potentially disrupting its function or reported metrics.

component_details.name: BeraHub (Frontend)
component_details.description: Web interface for managing $BGT, accessing liquidity, and participating in governance.

attack_surface.data_description: Governance proposal data including encoded actions

behavior_details.name: Offer Incentive Tokens
behavior_details.description: Protocol offers incentive tokens to a Reward Vault to attract BGT emissions.

data_flow_details.description: Protocol offers incentive tokens to a Reward Vault.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Maliciously crafted proposal actions could disrupt the governance process or execution phase.

actor_risk.description: An external user with sufficient BGT attempting to disrupt governance.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting vulnerabilities in proposal execution logic can halt or delay critical network upgrades or changes.

assumptions: The governance contract's execution logic for proposals is vulnerable to malformed or malicious input actions.
Guardian oversight or automated checks do not fully prevent disruptive proposals from reaching or failing in the execution phase.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rigorous validation and sandboxing for proposal execution, enhance guardian monitoring and emergency cancellation capabilities.

description: An attacker may create a malicious governance proposal designed to cause the execution phase to fail or disrupt the governance contract, leading to denial of service.

component_details.name: BeraHub (Frontend)
component_details.description: Web interface for managing $BGT, accessing liquidity, and participating in governance.

attack_surface.data_description: Governance proposal data aiming to alter parameters or contract ownership

behavior_details.name: Offer Incentive Tokens
behavior_details.description: Protocol offers incentive tokens to a Reward Vault to attract BGT emissions.

data_flow_details.description: Protocol offers incentive tokens to a Reward Vault.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: A malicious proposal could, if passed and executed, grant excessive privileges or alter critical system parameters unfairly.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to manipulate governance outcomes for illicit gain.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful exploitation allows attacker to gain unauthorized control or benefit by subverting governance.

assumptions: Voting power distribution is concentrated enough for a malicious actor/coalition to potentially meet quorum.
Guardian oversight or timelock delay is insufficient to detect and cancel all malicious proposals.
Vulnerabilities exist in how proposals are processed or executed that could be exploited for privilege escalation.

mitigation.strategy: Maintain decentralized BGT distribution, ensure active and vigilant guardians, implement robust timelock procedures, and conduct thorough code reviews of proposal actions.

description: An attacker may propose a governance change that, if successfully passed and executed, grants them unauthorized privileges or alters network parameters to their advantage.

asset_details.name: Private Key
asset_details.description: Cryptographic key used to access and control assets in a wallet.

behavior_details.name: Execute Governance Proposal
behavior_details.description: Governance contract executes the changes defined in a passed proposal on the blockchain.

data_flow_details.description: Validator proposes a new block to the chain.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: The BGT redemption mechanism might be vulnerable to attacks preventing users from converting BGT to BERA.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to disrupt the BGT redemption service.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disrupting the primary exit mechanism for BGT holders impacts user trust and network utility.

assumptions: The BGT redemption contract has vulnerabilities (e.g., reentrancy, resource limits) that can be exploited by an attacker.
The process linking BGT burn to BERA minting/transfer is susceptible to jamming.

mitigation.strategy: Thoroughly audit redemption contract logic, implement circuit breakers or rate limiting if necessary.

description: An attacker may attempt to cause a denial of service on the BGT to BERA redemption mechanism, preventing users from swapping their BGT.

component_details.name: HoneyFactory (Smart Contract)
component_details.description: Central smart contract hub for $HONEY minting, connecting vaults.

asset_details.name: Private Key
asset_details.description: Cryptographic key used to access and control assets in a wallet.

asset_details.name: Voting Power
asset_details.description: Influence in governance decisions, derived from BGT holdings/delegation.

behavior_details.name: Distribute Block Rewards
behavior_details.description: Berachain distributes BGT emissions and collected fees/MEV to the block-proposing validator.

data_flow_details.description: Governance contract executes a passed proposal on the chain.

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction visibility in the mempool allows malicious actors to front-run or sandwich swaps, affecting execution price.

actor_risk.description: A malicious actor (potentially a bot) monitoring the mempool for profitable swaps.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Mempool manipulation directly impacts the fairness and predictability of asset swaps, potentially causing significant losses for users.

assumptions: Transaction details are publicly broadcast before block inclusion.
Validators or block builders can reorder transactions to exploit price movements.

mitigation.strategy: Implement MEV-resistant transaction relay methods or protocol-level MEV smoothing/redistribution.

description: An attacker can front-run or sandwich legitimate swap transactions by observing them in the mempool, manipulating the price, and profiting at the expense of the user.

component_details.name: FeeCollector (Smart Contract)
component_details.description: Smart contract collecting dApp fees and distributing them to $BGT holders.

asset_details.name: Voting Power
asset_details.description: Influence in governance decisions, derived from BGT holdings/delegation.

behavior_details.name: Stake on Behalf of User
behavior_details.description: Developer stakes assets in a Reward Vault on behalf of a user.

data_flow_details.description: Incentive Token Manager adds or updates incentive tokens/rate in a Reward Vault.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Vulnerabilities or state dependencies in the mint/burn process, especially during Basket Mode transitions, could allow for temporary or permanent disruption.

actor_risk.description: An external user attempting to exploit stablecoin mechanics for disruption.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Disrupting the stablecoin's core function undermines its utility and trust in the ecosystem.

assumptions: The HoneySwap/Factory/Vault contracts contain vulnerabilities related to handling collateral state or Basket Mode logic.
Timing attacks or race conditions around Basket Mode activation/deactivation can disrupt minting or burning.

mitigation.strategy: Conduct extensive testing and auditing of stablecoin contract logic, particularly for edge cases and state transitions like Basket Mode.

description: An attacker may attempt to disrupt the HONEY minting or burning process, potentially by exploiting vulnerabilities related to collateral depegging or Basket Mode state changes.
